zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Anderton於1508年7月1日出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾可洛斯頓時，她的父親Hugh Anderton42歲，母親Joan30歲。她於1525年與Nicholas Rigby IV結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1535年8月在英國英格蘭藍卡爾享年27歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Anderton于1508年7月1日出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾可洛斯頓时，她的父亲Hugh Anderton42岁，母亲Joan30岁。她于1525年与Nicholas Rigby IV结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1535年8月在英国英格兰藍卡爾享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: Anderton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places so named, in Cheshire, Lancashire, and Devon. Those in Cheshire and Lancashire are named with the personal name Ēanrēd (Old English) or Eindrithi (Old Norse) + Old English tūn ‘settlement’. The Devon placename, recorded as Underdon in 1270, means ‘below the down’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Anderton was born on 1 July 1508, in Croston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh Anderton, was 42 and her mother, Joan, was 30. She married Nicholas Rigby IV in 1525. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in August 1535, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Anderton est née le 1 juillet 1508, à Croston, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh Anderton, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Joan, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas Rigby IV en 1525. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en août 1535, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Anderton am 1. Juli 1508 in Croston, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Anderton 42 und ihre Mutter Joan 30. Sie hat 1525 Nicholas Rigby IV geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb im August 1535 im Alter von 27 Jahren in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Anderton è nata il il 1 luglio 1508 nel Croston, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Hugh Anderton, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Joan, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Nicholas Rigby IV nel 1525. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel agosto 1535, nel Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 27 anni.
